source_intake_record.raw_question_text: An insulated tank has an air inlet, \( \omega_1 = 0.0084 \), and an outlet, \( T_2 = 22^\circ \mathrm{C}, \, \phi_2 = 90\% \), both at \( 100 \, \mathrm{kPa} \). A third line sprays \( 0.25 \, \mathrm{kg/s} \) water at \( 80^\circ \mathrm{C}, \, 100 \, \mathrm{kPa} \). For steady operation, find the required air inlet temperature, \( T_1 \).

Solving for T1 plausibly requires chained calculations rather than direct lookup, making it clearly multi-step
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a specific numeric target ('36.5 °C')
